facts.text: The village’s name, Interlaken, was chosen by schoolteacher Miss Georgiana Wheeler after her travels to Switzerland, winning a railroad contest for the depot’s new title.
In 2002 the First Baptist Church of Interlaken earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places, preserving its 19th‑century architecture.
The Interlaken Historical Society, founded in 1951, grew from a handful of locals into a North American network dedicated to preserving the village’s heritage.
Every September, Interlaken hosts the Plowing Day and Country Fair, a community event that attracts over 500 visitors with crafts, food, and history demos.
